_meta.why: Knowledge orchestration policy. Governs RAG retrieval, citation, freshness, fallback, and web search override behaviour.

retrieval_policy.why: top-5 at 0.65 threshold balances recall and precision for technical documentation. 2560 tokens (5 × 512) fits comfortably within the 65K context ceiling without crowding reasoning space.

freshness_policy.why: Technical documentation older than 6 months may reference deprecated APIs or outdated versions. Flagging stale content prevents confident presentation of superseded information.

citation_policy.mandatory_when: RAG chunk was used in the response
citation_policy.why: Citations allow users to verify claims and trace which knowledge source influenced an answer. Omitting them when RAG was used is a quality failure.

rag_mandatory_conditions: query explicitly references a loaded collection by name
query asks about a specific version, API, or library that has a collection loaded
query asks for code that must conform to a project-specific standard
rag_skip_conditions: query is conversational with no domain-specific terms
query asks for general computer science concepts covered in model training
RAG retrieval score < 0.65 for all chunks (fall back to model knowledge)

triggers: user asks for current version, price, or availability
user explicitly requests a web search
web_search_override.why: Web search overrides RAG when freshness is the primary concern. RAG collections are snapshots; web search is real-time.
